# Appendix: Debug walkthrough {#sec-debug}

Real preprocessing takes several minutes per run, which is painful when you
are iterating on the UI rather than processing new data. The debug walkthrough
lets you seed the pipeline once from a real recording, then drive every screen
instantly.

::: callout-note
This is a developer convenience, not part of a normal experiment session.
:::

## One-time seed

Seed the debug snapshots from a real recording:

```bash
python -m scripts.demo_seed_debug_snapshots --data <path/to/subject>
```

The snapshots are written under `debug_snapshots/`, which is **git-ignored** —
each developer regenerates their own. Re-run the seed command when you move to
a new machine.

## Drive the walkthrough

```bash
python -m frontend.debug.main
```

Step through the whole pipeline with **Ctrl+→**. A `--phase2` quick-jump entry
point is available to land directly on the live-inference screen.

See [`src/frontend/debug/README.md`](../../../src/frontend/debug/README.md)
for the full walkthrough mechanics.
